body:
Bead agent-63bf58a6 was decomposed rather than implemented in a single attempt.

## Why decomposed

The bead's AC list spans 7 deliverables across unrelated subsystems:

  AC1  per-provider state machine (routing core)
  AC2  burn-rate tracking + new config schema
  AC3  HTTP header parsing for three distinct providers
  AC4  periodic probe loop (concurrency / lifecycle)
  AC5  new typed error in the public error surface
  AC6  tests for all of the above
  AC7  CONTRACT-003 documentation amendment

Per the executor's size check ("More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated
subsystems" → decompose, do not attempt), this triggered decomposition.
Each slice below is independently testable, has tight AC, and a clear
boundary; serializing them lets each pass through review on its own
merits.

## Children created (with bead IDs)

  fizeau-92b4b823
    Title: fizeau: provider quota state machine + NoViableProviderForNow error
    Scope: AC1 (state machine + candidate-selection exclusion) and AC5
    (NoViableProviderForNow typed error).  Foundational; siblings
    depend on it.

  fizeau-50b14297
    Title: fizeau: rate-limit header parsing for Anthropic, OpenAI,
           OpenRouter -> quota_exhausted transition
    Scope: AC3 — header parsers for the three providers, wired into
    dispatch HTTP path, transitions provider via the state machine.
    Depends on fizeau-92b4b823.

  fizeau-f2661619
    Title: fizeau: per-provider burn-rate tracker + daily_token_budget
           config + predictive take-out
    Scope: AC2 — rolling-window token tracker + new operator config
    knob (providers.<name>.daily_token_budget) + predictive transition.
    Depends on fizeau-92b4b823.

  .execute-bead-wt-agent-63bf58a6-20260502T192505-092f5e0d-5208511b
    Title: fizeau: periodic HealthCheck probe restores quota_exhausted
           providers
    Scope: AC4 — probe loop extending service_probe.go, restores
    quota_exhausted providers on probe success, bounded backoff on
    failure.  Depends on fizeau-92b4b823.

  .execute-bead-wt-agent-63bf58a6-20260502T192505-092f5e0d-78f47557
    Title: fizeau: amend CONTRACT-003 for provider quota state,
           NoViableProviderForNow, and operator config knobs
    Scope: AC7 — contract amendment.  Done after the implementation
    beads land so the contract reflects shipped behavior.  Depends on
    all four implementation beads.

AC6 (tests) is folded into each child's AC list — every child carries
its own unit-test coverage.

(Note: the last two child IDs got a long cwd-derived prefix from
`ddx bead create`'s auto-prefix logic; they are otherwise normal
beads and addressable by the full ID.)

## Dependency edges recorded

  fizeau-92b4b823               -> agent-63bf58a6 (parent)
  fizeau-50b14297               -> agent-63bf58a6, fizeau-92b4b823
  fizeau-f2661619               -> agent-63bf58a6, fizeau-92b4b823
  ...probe-bead-...-5208511b    -> agent-63bf58a6, fizeau-92b4b823
  ...docs-bead-...-78f47557     -> agent-63bf58a6, fizeau-92b4b823,
                                   fizeau-50b14297, fizeau-f2661619,
                                   ...probe-bead-...-5208511b

Parent (agent-63bf58a6) was annotated with the decomposition via
`ddx bead update --notes` and is left open until children land.

## What a follow-up needs

Pick fizeau-92b4b823 first (it unblocks the others).  Once it merges,
fizeau-50b14297 / fizeau-f2661619 / the probe bead can be drained in
parallel.  The CONTRACT-003 amendment bead runs last because its AC
references "shipped behavior."

No code changes were made in this execution; no commit is produced.
